title: 10 best herbal creams for premenstrual syndrome

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain angelica archangelica, a natural herb that has been used for centuries to alleviate symptoms associated with PMS. The cream's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties help to reduce breast tenderness and menstrual cramps, while its estrogen-regulating effects can help balance hormones and ease mood swings and anxiety. Additionally, angelica creams often contain calming essential oils like lavender, which can promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, further alleviating PMS symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Angelica's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling and pain associated with PMS symptoms such as cramps, bloating, and breast tenderness.
Antispasmodic properties: The spasmolytic compounds in angelica creams relax uterine muscles, alleviating menstrual cramps and spasms, which can be intense during PMS.

Hormone-balancing properties: Angelica's flavonoids and terpenes help regulate hormonal fluctuations, mitigating symptoms of PMS such as mood swings, irritability, and anxiety caused by estrogen imbalances.
remedy_parts: Roots: Angelica roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them an effective ingredient in creams to alleviate cramps and bloating associated with PMS.

Rhyzomes: The rhizome of angelica is rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm mood swings and anxiety common during premenstrual syndrome.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried angelica archangelica root and mix it with 20 grams of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes to allow the herb's bioactive compounds to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the oil from the solids and discard the solids.
Mix the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and heat it at 70°c for an additional 10 minutes until the wax is fully melted.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass containers and let it cool and solidify before using as a topical cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pink peony creams helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain natural ingredients that have a profound impact on hormone regulation. The herbal extracts, such as Dong Quai and Chasteberry, work to balance the body's estrogen levels, alleviating symptoms like bloating, mood swings, and breast tenderness. Additionally, the creams' anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe cramps and joint pain, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. By addressing the root causes of PMS, pink peony creams provide relief from the physical and emotional discomfort associated with this common condition.
remedy_properties: Estrogen-regulating compounds: The cream's estrogen-balancing properties can help reduce PMS symptoms such as mood swings, breast tenderness, and bloating by regulating hormonal imbalances.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agents: The herbal extracts in the cream have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate physical symptoms of PMS like cramps, headaches, and fatigue by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Mood-enhancing compounds: The cream's mood-regulating compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, can help alleviate emotional symptoms of PMS like anxiety, depression, and irritability by promoting relaxation, calming the mind, and regulating neurotransmitters.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and mood swings associated with PMS.
Flowers: Contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the body, reducing anxiety and irritability during premenstrual phases.
remedy_recipe: Harvest paeonia officinalis roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is mature.
Dry the harvested roots in a cool dark place for at least two weeks to reduce moisture content.
Grind 10 grams of dried paeonia officinalis root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered root with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 20 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with premenstrual syndrome because they provide a natural solution to alleviate symptoms such as anxiety, insomnia, and mood swings. The valerenic acids in these creams interact with the brain's neurotransmitters, promoting relaxation and reducing stress. Additionally, valerian cream can help regulate hormones, leading to improved sleep quality and reduced irritability. As a result, women experiencing premenstrual syndrome can find relief from physical and emotional discomforts, allowing them to manage their symptoms more effectively and improve overall well-being during this time of the month.
remedy_properties: Sedative and Anti-Anxiety Properties: Valerian's calming effects can help alleviate anxiety, restlessness, and mood swings associated with PMS, allowing women to feel more relaxed and centered.

Antispasmodic and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Valerian's anti-inflammatory compounds can help soothe cramps, bloating, and breast tenderness often experienced during the premenstrual phase, providing relief from physical discomfort.

Adaptogenic and Stress-Reducing Properties: Valerian's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to stress, which is common during PMS, and reduce symptoms like irritability, mood swings, and fatigue, promoting overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain a high concentration of essential oils and sesquiterpenes, which help to soothe cramps and reduce anxiety associated with PMS.
Fruits: Valerian fruits have been traditionally used to ease menstrual cramps, bloating, and mood swings due to their flavonoid content and ability to relax the uterine muscles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots, wash them thoroughly, and dry them in a warm place for at least 7 days.
Combine dried valeriana officinalis roots with 200 grams of coconut oil and 100 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the ingredients are fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat, let it cool to room temperature, and whip it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars, label them, and store them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree creams helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain a natural extract that regulates hormonal imbalances, particularly estrogen. This cream works by stimulating the pituitary gland to produce progesterone, which helps alleviate symptoms such as bloating, mood swings, and breast tenderness associated with PMS. Additionally, chaste tree creams may help reduce anxiety and stress related to premenstrual syndrome by promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. As a result, women who use these creams often experience significant relief from their premenstrual symptoms.
remedy_properties: Vitex agnus-castus extract: Relieves PMS symptoms such as breast tenderness, mood swings, and anxiety by regulating hormonal imbalances, particularly prolactin levels.
Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to PMS symptoms like bloating, cramps, and fatigue.
Dopamine-modulating activity: Helps regulate mood and emotional fluctuations associated with PMS by interacting with dopamine receptors and modulating serotonin levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in iridoid glycosides, such as aucubin and casticin, which help regulate hormones and alleviate PMS symptoms.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, reducing cramps and bloating associated with PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 100g of fresh vitex agnus-castus leaves and stems for later use.
Combine 50g of dried vitex agnus-castus herb with 150ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds from the herbs.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids before mixing with 50g of beeswax and 25g of vitamin e oil.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain glycyrrhizin, a natural compound that has been shown to reduce symptoms of PMS such as bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help to alleviate cramps and discomfort associated with menstruation. Additionally, licorice root has been found to regulate estrogen levels, which can further contribute to alleviating PMS symptoms. As a result, using an herbal licorice cream may provide relief from the uncomfortable and debilitating symptoms of PMS.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as breast tenderness, bloating, and mood swings associated with PMS by suppressing prostaglandins and reducing uterine contractions.

Flavonoids: Possess antioxidant properties that help to regulate hormonal imbalances and reduce the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which can contribute to symptoms like fatigue, mood changes, and joint pain during PMS.

Saponins: Have anti-spasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects that may help to relieve cramping, bloating, and other physical symptoms associated with PMS by relaxing smooth muscle contractions and reducing uterine inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps alleviate PMS symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
Barks: Contain triterpenoid saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing PMS-related cramps and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry glycyrrhiza glabra roots in shade at room temperature for 2-3 weeks.
Grind dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 20% w/w powdered glycyrrhiza glabra with 80% w/w coconut oil in stainless steel bowl for 10 minutes.
Heat mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes and stir occasionally to combine ingredients evenly.
Fill sterilized cream tubes or jars with cooled mixture and store in refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort creams help with premenstrual syndrome because they contain hyperforin, a natural ingredient that has been shown to alleviate symptoms of PMS. The cream works by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation in the body, which can help to ease symptoms such as bloating, mood swings, and breast tenderness. By applying the cream topically, the active ingredients are able to penetrate directly into the skin, providing quick and effective relief from PMS symptoms without the need for oral medication. This makes it an attractive option for women seeking a natural and convenient solution.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with PMS, which can alleviate symptoms such as breast tenderness and mood swings.
Antidepressant properties: Can help to regulate serotonin levels in the brain, which may alleviate symptoms of anxiety, irritability, and depression that are common during PMS.
Anti-inflammatory effects: May reduce inflammation and pain associated with physical symptoms of PMS such as cramps, bloating, and breast swelling, allowing for improved overall comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome, such as anxiety and mood swings.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing menstrual cramps and breast tenderness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh st john's wort leaves and flowers, wash them thoroughly in cold water.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 6 hours or air dry for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, measuring out 20g of the powdered hypericum perforatum.
Combine the powdered herb with 100ml of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler, heating the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally.

Remove the mixture from heat, let it cool to room temperature, then whip it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer and transferring it to sterile glass jars, allowing it to infuse for at least 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with premenstrual syndrome because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the mind and body. The herb's calming properties, such as rosmarinic acid and terpenes, work to reduce stress and anxiety, common symptoms associated with PMS. Additionally, lemon balm's anti-inflammatory effects help to ease bloating, cramps, and mood swings, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating menstrual discomfort. By applying the cream topically, its benefits are quickly absorbed into the skin, providing fast-acting relief from PMS symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm's antispasmodic property helps to relax smooth muscles and reduce cramps associated with PMS, providing relief from abdominal pain and discomfort.

Anxiolytic property: The anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects of lemon balm help to calm the nervous system, alleviating symptoms such as anxiety, irritability, and mood swings often experienced during PMS.

Antihistaminic property: Lemon balm's antihistaminic activity helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as breast tenderness, bloating, and swelling associated with water retention and hormonal fluctuations during PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in rosmarinic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and soothe cramps associated with PMS.
Leaves: Abundant in vitamin B6, which plays a crucial role in regulating hormonal balances and alleviating symptoms of PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and dry them using air circulation at room temperature for two weeks.
Crush dried melissa officinalis leaves into a fine powder using mortar and pestle to obtain 20 grams of extract.
Combine 10 grams of the melissa officinalis powder with 30 grams of beeswax in a double boiler to melt the wax.
Add 40 grams of sweet almond oil and stir until the mixture reaches a temperature of 60 degrees celsius while continuing to heat for one hour.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature before transferring to sterilized jars for storage and use within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams help with premenstrual syndrome because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that effectively alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and mood swings. The active ingredients in ginger, including gingerols and shogaols, work to reduce prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances responsible for menstrual cramp pain and swelling. Additionally, the creams' warming properties help to relax muscles, further reducing discomfort. By providing a soothing and calming effect on the body, herbal ginger creams offer a natural solution for managing PMS symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation and ease PMS-related pelvic pain and cramps.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in ginger cream, including vitamin C and E, protect the body against oxidative stress and damage caused by hormonal fluctuations during PMS, reducing symptoms like breast tenderness and mood swings.
Digestive soothing properties: Ginger's ability to relax the digestive tract and reduce inflammation can alleviate common PMS symptoms like bloating, cramps, and nausea.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizome, or underground stem, contains active compounds like gingerol and shogaol that have anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in relieving menstrual cramps.
Roots: Ginger roots are a rich source of essential oils and compounds that help reduce nausea and digestive issues often associated with premenstrual syndrome.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality zingiber officinale roots and clean them thoroughly for future use.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm place for two weeks or until crispy and brittle.
Grind 100g of dried zingiber officinale root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground root with 200ml of coconut oil, 50ml of beeswax, and 20 drops of lavender essential oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes or until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats creams helps with premenstrual syndrome because they provide a soothing and calming relief to the skin, reducing inflammation and itching associated with PMS. The natural anti-inflammatory properties of oats help to ease symptoms such as breast tenderness, mood swings, and digestive issues. Additionally, the creamy texture of oat creams provides hydration and nourishment to the skin, promoting relaxation and reducing stress. As a result, herbal oat creams can be an effective way to alleviate the physical and emotional discomforts associated with PMS.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal oats creams containing anti-inflammatory compounds like avenanthramides can help reduce PMS symptoms such as bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings by decreasing inflammation in the body.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of herbal oats creams, including vitamins E and K, can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage caused by hormonal fluctuations during PMS, leading to reduced symptoms like cramps and fatigue.

Soothing and calming effects: The natural astringent and emollient properties of oat extracts in herbal creams can provide relief from skin irritation and discomfort associated with PMS, such as acne, itchiness, and skin rashes, by soothing and calming the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe cramps associated with PMS.
Barks: Contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in alleviating symptoms of PMS such as mood swings and breast tenderness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality avena sativa oats and dry them in a cool dark place for at least 24 hours to preserve potency.
Grind 100 grams of dried oat powder into a fine paste using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, then sift through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any lumps.
Combine the oat paste with 20 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed oil, and stir well for at least 5 minutes until fully incorporated.
Add 10 grams of beeswax pellets and heat the mixture in a double boiler set over simmering water for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, to create a smooth and creamy consistency.
Remove the cream from heat, pour into sterilized glass jars, and allow it to cool and thicken at room temperature for at least 2 hours before use or storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea creams helps with premenstrual syndrome because they harness the natural benefits of herbs to soothe and calm the body. Herbal tea creams, rich in flavonoids and antioxidants, can help alleviate symptoms such as breast tenderness, mood swings, and bloating by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. The topical application of these creams allows for rapid absorption into the skin, providing quick relief from PMS discomforts and promoting a sense of calm and well-being. This natural approach can be a valuable addition to a premenstrual routine.
remedy_properties: Chamomile: Soothes digestive issues and anxiety symptoms associated with PMS by calming the nervous system and reducing inflammation in the gut.
Lavender: Helps to reduce cramps, bloating, and breast tenderness by relaxing muscle tissue, improving circulation, and reducing stress hormones.
Vitamin B-rich herbs like St. John's Wort and Dong Quai: Support hormone regulation and balance, which can alleviate symptoms such as mood swings, fatigue, and irritability.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the tea plant are used due to their high concentration of alkaloids, which help to alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome such as anxiety and depression.
Barks: The barks of the tea plant contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with premenstrual syndrome.
Seeds: Tea seeds are rich in omega-6 fatty acids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome such as breast tenderness and fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality green tea leaves at dawn and dry them in the shade for 2 hours before processing.
Combine 20 grams of dried camellia sinensis leaves with 100 milliliters of boiling water, steeping for 10 minutes to create a strong infusion.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil with 30 milliliters of the infused camellia sinensis liquid in a double boiler, heating until melted and blended.
Remove from heat, pour into sterilized glass jars, and allow to cool and set at room temperature for 60 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with premenstrual syndrome because of its calming and relaxing properties. The essential oils present in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have a sedative effect on the body, which can help reduce stress, anxiety, and mood swings that often accompany PMS symptoms. Additionally, lavender's anti-inflammatory properties may alleviate cramps and bloating associated with menstruation. As a result, using English lavender creams during the premenstrual phase can promote overall well-being and provide relief from discomforts.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (calming) properties: Lavender's anxiolytic properties can help alleviate anxiety and stress associated with PMS, promoting relaxation and reducing symptoms like mood swings and irritability.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation and soothe physical symptoms of PMS, such as breast tenderness, bloating, and cramps, providing relief and comfort.

Hormone-balancing properties: Lavender's estrogenic and progesteronic activities may help regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to PMS symptoms, promoting a more balanced reproductive system and reducing symptoms like mood changes, fatigue, and digestive issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lavender roots help reduce symptoms of PMS such as bloating and cramps.
Leaves: Containing calming essential oils like linalool and linalyl acetate, lavender leaves soothe anxiety and emotional mood swings associated with PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in late summer or early fall for optimal potency and flavor.
Mix 20 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for eight hours or overnight to extract the active compounds and essential oils.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids and retaining 50 milliliters of the infused oil.
Blend the infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut cream, heating the mixture gently until it reaches a temperature of 160°f to create a smooth and creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Summer savory creams help with premenstrual syndrome because they contain natural ingredients that ease physical symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and breast tenderness. The herbs in these creams, like chamomile and lavender, also promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, which are common emotional challenges during PMS. Additionally, summer savory creams can help regulate hormones and balance the body's natural rhythms, leading to a reduction in mood swings and irritability.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Summer savory contains antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, alleviating PMS symptoms such as bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings.

Sedative and calming effects: Summer savory's essential oils have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm anxiety, restlessness, and irritability associated with PMS, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels.

Pain-relieving properties: The volatile oils present in summer savory have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate cramps, headaches, and body aches that often accompany PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate cramps and discomfort associated with PMS.
Seeds: Rich in omega-3 fatty acids, which can reduce inflammation and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried satureja hortensis leaves in a carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil for at least 4 weeks.
Combine the infused oil with beeswax and shea butter to create a base cream mixture weighing approximately 200 grams.
Add a few drops of essential oils including clary sage and lavender to enhance relaxation properties and improve skin absorption.
Whip the mixture using an electric mixer or hand blender for about 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and creamy.
Pour the final mixture into sterilized jars and seal tightly, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature for approximately 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain natural compounds that ease digestive discomfort, bloating, and cramping associated with PMS. The herb fennel has a calming effect on the muscles, reducing spasms and contractions that can exacerbate cramps. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate breast tenderness and swelling. By targeting these physical symptoms, herbal fennel creams provide natural relief for women experiencing premenstrual discomfort, promoting a sense of well-being and normalcy leading up to their period.
remedy_properties: Estrogen-regulating flavonoids: Fennel cream's estrogen-regulating flavonoids help to balance hormonal fluctuations, reducing symptoms such as bloating and mood swings associated with PMS.
Carminative and anti-inflammatory terpenes: The carminative and anti-inflammatory properties of fennel's terpenes help to alleviate digestive issues like cramps and discomfort, common PMS symptoms.

Antioxidant and anti-anxiety phenolic acids: Fennel cream's antioxidant and anti-anxiety phenolic acids may help reduce stress and anxiety associated with PMS by promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have been traditionally used to soothe menstrual cramps and reduce bloating due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain anethole, a natural compound that has been shown to reduce symptoms of PMS such as anxiety, irritability, and mood swings.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried foeniculum vulgare seeds and carrier oils such as coconut or sweet almond oil in sufficient quantities.
Combine 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare seeds with 100 milliliters of carrier oil in a glass jar and steep for four hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids after filtering.
Add 10 grams of beeswax to the filtered liquid and heat at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes until fully dissolved.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and solidify before use within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Nutmeg creams helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain natural ingredients that alleviate symptoms associated with PMS. The cream's active compounds, such as myristicin and elemicin, have a calming effect on the body, reducing anxiety, depression, and mood swings. Additionally, nutmeg's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe cramps and bloating, while its antioxidant properties protect the body from free radical damage caused by hormonal fluctuations. As a result, using herbal nutmeg creams can provide relief from PMS symptoms and promote overall well-being during this time of the month.
remedy_properties: Magnesium content: Magnesium in nutmeg cream helps to relax muscles, reduce cramps, and ease anxiety associated with PMS.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Nutmeg's antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation, which can contribute to bloating, breast tenderness, and mood swings during PMS, while its anti-inflammatory compounds alleviate pain and discomfort.

Volatile oils and terpenes: The volatile oils and terpenes in nutmeg, such as myristicin and limonene, have a sedative effect, helping to calm nervousness, reduce irritability, and promote relaxation during PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the nutmeg plant contain a compound called myristicin, which has been shown to reduce symptoms of premenstrual syndrome such as bloating and mood swings.
Barks: The bark of the nutmeg plant is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help to alleviate cramps, fatigue, and other physical symptoms associated with PMS.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried myristica fragrans powder in sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:4 for 48 hours.
Combine the infused oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a double boiler and melt at medium heat for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids after 15 minutes.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer for 2 minutes.
Transfer the whipped cream to glass jars and store in the refrigerator at 40°f for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild rose creams helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain soothing properties that ease the discomfort associated with PMS. The creams' anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agents help to calm irritated skin, reduce breast tenderness, and alleviate cramps. The natural emollients also help to moisturize dry skin and reduce bloating, leaving women feeling more relaxed and comfortable throughout their menstrual cycle. Additionally, the calming scent of rose can promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, further alleviating symptoms associated with PMS.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory properties: Wild rose creams help to dilate blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and alleviating symptoms like headaches, breast tenderness, and fatigue associated with PMS.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds in wild rose cream help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to mood swings, bloating, and cramps during PMS.

Hormone-regulating properties: Wild rose is believed to have a regulatory effect on hormonal imbalances, which is thought to help alleviate symptoms like mood changes, irritability, and anxiety associated with PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help alleviate PMS symptoms by reducing inflammation and improving hormonal balance.
Fruits: Contain vitamins A and C, as well as potassium and magnesium, making them beneficial for soothing cramps and alleviating mood swings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rose petals and calendula flowers and dry them in shade for at least 24 hours.
Grind 100 grams of dried rose petals and 50 grams of dried calendula flowers into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered mixture with 200 milliliters of sweet almond oil and heat it gently over low flame for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids, then add 20 drops of lavender essential oil and mix well.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars, label them clearly, and store in refrigerator for up to three months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with premenstrual syndrome because of its ability to ease symptoms such as cramps, bloating, and mood swings. The cream's antispasmodic properties help relax uterine muscles, reducing cramp severity. Thyme also possesses anti-inflammatory compounds that may reduce bloating and breast tenderness. Additionally, the calming effects of thyme can help alleviate irritability and anxiety associated with PMS. By applying the cream topically, women can experience fast and effective relief from premenstrual discomfort, allowing them to manage their symptoms naturally.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to PMS symptoms like cramps, bloating, and mood swings.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, may help alleviate physical symptoms of PMS, including breast tenderness, headaches, and fatigue.

Muscle-relaxant properties: The essential oils present in thyme, particularly borneol, can help relax smooth muscle tissue, which may provide relief from cramps, spasms, and other physical discomforts associated with PMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of PMS such as bloating and cramping.
Leaves: The leaves of the thyme plant contain antioxidants and essential oils that may help reduce stress and anxiety associated with PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulagris leaves, then wash and dry them thoroughly with paper towels.
Combine 250g of dried thymus vulagris leaves with 500ml of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes to create a strong infusion.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, discarding solids and reserving liquid.
Mix 100g of unscented petroleum jelly with 20ml of the thymus vulagris infusion in a double boiler, heating until smooth and uniform.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena creams helps with premenstrual syndrome because of its unique combination of calming and uplifting properties. The herb lemon verbena has a natural ability to soothe the nervous system, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress that often accompany PMS symptoms. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate physical discomfort such as bloating and cramping, while its moisturizing effects can calm dry skin and irritations commonly experienced during this time.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon verbena cream helps to relieve PMS-related cramps, bloating, and breast tenderness by reducing muscle spasms and easing digestive discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon verbena cream reduce swelling and pain associated with PMS symptoms such as joint pain, headaches, and mood swings.
Anxiolytic (calming) properties: Lemon verbena's calming effects help to alleviate anxiety, irritability, and emotional ups and downs often experienced during the premenstrual phase.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe cramps and reduce pain associated with PMS.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, barks have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and mood swings during PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers in abundance.
Dry the herb material using a food dehydrator at low temperature for 8 hours.
Combine 100g of dried verbena officinalis with 500ml of distilled water in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes before filtering out the solids and transferring to an amber bottle.
Mix 200g of beeswax with 400ml of filtered infusion and heat gently until the wax is fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with premenstrual syndrome because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that alleviate cramps, bloating, and breast tenderness associated with PMS. The creams also possess natural hormonal balancers that regulate estrogen levels, reducing symptoms of anxiety, mood swings, and irritability. Additionally, stinging nettle's analgesic properties provide relief from pelvic pain and discomfort, making it an effective natural remedy for women experiencing PMS symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, help reduce swelling and pain associated with PMS symptoms like breast tenderness and bloating.
Hormone-balancing properties: The herb's estrogenic and progesteronic effects can help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of PMS, including mood swings, anxiety, and irritability.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle cream's high antioxidant content helps protect the body from free radical damage and reduces inflammation in the body, which can contribute to PMS symptoms like fatigue, cramps, and digestive issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of stinging nettle help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with PMS.
Seeds: Containing high levels of omega-3 fatty acids, stinging nettle seeds have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe menstrual cramps and breast tenderness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of urtica dioica plants in early morning or evening when essential oils are most concentrated.
Dry the harvested leaves using shade drying or air circulation for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using mortar and pestle, then sift through mesh screen to create uniform size particles.
Mix 30 grams of the powdered urtica dioica with 60 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of sweet almond oil in double boiler, heating at low temperature for 15-20 minutes.
Remove from heat, let cool slightly, then whip the mixture using electric mixer until it thickens to desired consistency and texture.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for premenstrual syndrome are natural topical remedies designed to alleviate symptoms associated with PMS, such as breast tenderness, mood swings, and bloating. These creams contain a blend of herbal extracts that work together to reduce inflammation, regulate hormones, and promote relaxation. Examples of effective herbal creams include those containing Dong Quai, Chasteberry, and Passionflower. By using these creams, women can experience improved symptom management, reduced anxiety and irritability, and enhanced overall well-being during their menstrual cycles.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat premenstrual syndrome with herbal creams is by using topical applications rich in natural oils and essential fatty acids. Chamomile, lavender, and calendula creams help alleviate cramps, bloating, and mood swings. Dong quai and ginseng creams also reduce hot flashes and breast tenderness. Additionally, evening primrose oil and St. John's Wort creams soothe anxiety and insomnia, promoting a sense of calm and well-being during the premenstrual phase.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to premenstrual syndrome that are treated with herbal creams are menopausal symptoms, such as hot flashes and night sweats. Other conditions that may be alleviated by herbal creams include menstrual cramps, fibromyalgia, and migraines. Herbal ingredients like valerian root, chamomile, and lavender have a calming effect on the body, while others like ginger and turmeric provide anti-inflammatory relief. These natural ingredients can help to reduce symptoms and promote relaxation without the side effects of pharmaceutical treatments.
